description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with the global docker:git image and a docker:dind service, defining three stages: build, package, and deploy. Add a unami-build job in the build stage that uses the registry.gitlab.com/i-am-root/drupal-builder:latest image, runs only on master, release*, feature*, and hotfix* branches, and executes echo "Build for demo purpose". Add a docker-package-ephemeral job in the package stage that also uses the drupal-builder image, has the tag dockerbuild, runs on the same branch patterns, and runs echo ${VERSION}, tar --exclude="./deployment" -czf deployment/docker/app/package.tar.gz ., docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_BUILD_TOKEN $CI_REGISTRY, docker build -t $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}_${CI_COMMIT_SHA} ./deployment/docker/app, and docker push $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}_${CI_COMMIT_SHA}. Add a k8s-deploy-ephemeral job in the deploy stage that uses the alpine image, targets the develop environment, is triggered manually, runs on the same branch patterns, installs curl, downloads and installs kubectl, configures the cluster with $KUBE_URL, $KUBE_USER, $KUBE_PASSWORD, substitutes CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG into the Kubernetes manifest files, updates the image reference to $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}_${CI_COMMIT_SHA}, and applies the namespace, pvc, mysql, and drupal manifests with kubectl. No caches or artifacts are defined.
